title: Escalation calibrated to decision substance: interrupt / flag-async / record-silent, tuned by gate verdicts
context: Plan 1 retro: the agent prompted for a merge that had nothing contestable in it (fast-forward, local, reversible, fully verified). Colin's constraint: do not bias progress over input, and do not bias arbitrary approval when there is nothing substantial to approve. Empty prompts train rubber-stamping, which degrades the signal value of real gates.

options.name: always-gate
options.rationale: Every workflow transition prompts the human; zero unreviewed compounding.
options.tradeoff: Human latency idles the pipeline; arbitrary approvals train rubber-stamping and erode gate signal.

options.name: always-proceed
options.rationale: Agent never interrupts; every call is an AgDR batched at gates; maximal throughput.
options.tradeoff: Progress bias: genuinely contested calls execute before input; review burden relocates downstream (PHI-030's warning).

options.name: substance-tiered
options.rationale: Interrupt-worthiness computed from fields already on every AgDR: confidence x blast_radius x reversibility. Tier 1 interrupt-blocking: hard-escalation domains (security, prod deploys, secrets, frozen contracts), evidence-ties research cannot break, low confidence with cross-cutting blast radius, changes to the oversight contract itself, publish/destroy actions. Tier 2 flag-async: medium confidence or contestable-but-reversible; AgDR filed as pending-review, pushed through the notification channel, work proceeds; overturn bounded by branch revert. Tier 3 record-silent: high confidence, local blast radius, verified reversibility; AgDR rides to the gate review profile.
options.tradeoff: Tier-2 work may execute on a path later overturned; calibration depends on honest agent self-assessment until verdict history accumulates.

steelman.strongest_case: No miscalibrated confidence can ever slip a wrong compounding decision past the human; simplest possible contract.
Gates already exist and are binding; batching all review there maximizes throughput and keeps exactly one review surface.

reasoning: The escalation test is decision substance, not action category. A local reversible action with contested substance deserves an interrupt; a publish-adjacent action with one sensible answer does not deserve ceremony. The middle tier delivers interruption-without-idling: the human is pinged and may intervene, but the pipeline never waits on their latency. Self-calibrating: AgDR verdict history at gates tunes the thresholds - overturned silent records raise the escalation bar, waved-through interrupts lower it. Same data as the earned-autonomy ratchet; no new machinery.
blast_radius: Orchestration-layer policy only; no engine code change. Shapes Plan 2 (pending-review AgDRs must route through the notification digest) and Plan 3 (worker-skill escalation logic implements the three tiers).

feedback.note: Amendment: self-assessed escalation tiers are BOOTSTRAP MODE ONLY. As soon as a functional Switchboard is stood up, tier judgment (confidence, blast radius, interrupt-worthiness) must come from an independent agent — fresh context, different model than the author, same independence rule as the verification lane (PHI-030). The judge reviews tier assignments on filed AgDRs: it can promote record-silent to flag-async (send the ping) or pause dependents on a call that should have blocked. Closure condition: Plan 3 routes tier assignment through the verifier-class judge; the M0 exit bar exercises it.
